title: [Blog]: Governed Agent Distribution Is Becoming the Enterprise Battleground
body:
## Thesis
The next phase of enterprise agent adoption is not just about model capability. It is about governed distribution: connectors, permissions, marketplaces, and admin controls that make agents deployable inside real enterprises.

## Audience
Enterprise architects, platform engineers, and technical leaders building internal AI platforms.

## Why this matters now
Recent enterprise agent launches highlight that enterprises need managed distribution, access control, and admin supervision for agents—not just smarter assistants.

## Key points to cover
- Why connectors and marketplaces change enterprise agent architecture
- Why governed distribution matters more than raw capability
- The relationship between plugins, permissions, and policy enforcement
- How to design safe internal agent ecosystems

## Required diagrams
- Enterprise agent distribution architecture
- Connector / permission / policy flow
- Admin control plane for internal agents

## Constraints
- Focus on architecture and governance
- Avoid generic product-comparison framing
- Draft only
- Add TODO markers for any fact-check items

## Done condition
Open a draft PR with:
- the post draft
- inline SVG diagrams for each major workflow
- suggested internal links
- a list of fact-check items

title: [Blog]: The New AI Interface Layer Is the Real Enterprise Battleground
body:
## Thesis
The competitive layer in enterprise AI is shifting upward—from model quality alone to workflow shells, collaboration surfaces, and the interface layer where humans and agents actually coordinate work.

## Audience
Enterprise architects, digital workplace leaders, AI platform teams, and senior engineers.

## Why this matters now
The newest AI competition is increasingly about who owns the operational interface layer where agents, approvals, documents, and workflows come together.

## Key points to cover
- Why the interface layer matters more than the model layer in enterprise adoption
- The role of approvals, memory, context, and collaboration surfaces
- How agent shells become control planes
- What enterprises should copy and what they should avoid

## Required diagrams
- AI interface-layer architecture
- Human-agent collaboration loop
- Control-plane boundaries across app, workflow, and model layers

## Constraints
- Keep the post architecture-first
- Avoid superficial vendor-comparison framing
- Draft only
- Add TODO markers for any fact-check items

## Done condition
Open a draft PR with:
- the post draft
- inline SVG diagrams for each major workflow
- suggested internal links
- a list of fact-check items
